metadata.title: Responsive Mobile Design and Touch Interactions

story.asA: photographer using Recipe on mobile devices
story.iWant: a fully responsive interface optimized for mobile, tablet, and desktop screens
story.soThat: I can convert presets on any device without usability issues like tiny buttons or horizontal scrolling
story.tasks: Task 1: Add Viewport Meta Tag and Mobile-First CSS Reset (AC-5, AC-6) Task 2: Implement Mobile Hero Section (<768px) (AC-1) Task 3: Implement Responsive Format Badges (AC-1, AC-2, AC-3) Task 4: Implement Responsive File Card Grid (AC-1, AC-2, AC-3) Task 5: Implement Touch-Friendly Tap Targets (AC-4) Task 6: Implement Mobile Upload Drop Zone (AC-1) Task 7: Test on iPhone (iOS Safari) (AC-7) Task 8: Test on Android (Chrome) (AC-7) Task 9: Test on iPad (Safari) (AC-7) Task 10: Test on Desktop Browsers (AC-7) Task 11: Manual Testing Documentation

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: AC-1: Mobile Layout (<768px) - Single-column stacked layout, readable text without zooming, full-width buttons, no drag-and-drop (tap-to-browse only) AC-2: Tablet Layout (768px-1023px) - Two-column grid for file cards, 2x3 format badge grid, optimized hero section AC-3: Desktop Layout (>1024px) - Three-column grid for file cards, horizontal format badge row, full-size hero section AC-4: Touch-Friendly Tap Targets - Minimum 44px height (iOS guidelines), 8px spacing between targets AC-5: No Horizontal Scrolling - Viewport meta tag, all content fits within viewport, responsive images/SVGs, test on iPhone SE (320px) AC-6: Readable Text Without Zooming - Body text minimum 16px, line-height 1.5 (WCAG AA), color contrast 4.5:1 AC-7: Manual Testing on Real Devices - iPhone (iOS Safari), Android (Chrome), iPad, Desktop browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge)

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Enhanced Web UI/UX
doc.snippet: Responsive design with mobile-first CSS approach. Breakpoints: 320px (mobile), 768px (tablet), 1024px (desktop). Touch targets minimum 44px, WCAG AA compliance for text and contrast.

doc.title: Product Requirements Document (Path A)
doc.section: Epic 3: Enhanced Web UI/UX - Mobile-Responsive Design
doc.snippet: Mobile-responsive interface works on phones, tablets, and desktops. Touch-friendly interactions, no horizontal scrolling, modern interface that feels polished on all devices.

doc.title: Story 2-10: Responsive Design (Epic 2 - Complete)
doc.section: Mobile-first CSS implementation, touch detection, breakpoints
doc.snippet: Implemented responsive design for Epic 2 web interface with mobile-first CSS approach. Base mobile styles (<768px), tablet (@media min-width: 768px), desktop (@media min-width: 1024px). Touch targets 48x48px (WCAG AAA), orientation handling, touch detection via multiple browser APIs.

doc.title: Architecture
doc.snippet: Recipe uses vanilla JavaScript (ES6+) with no frameworks for zero build step and simple deployment. CSS uses custom properties for theming, mobile-first responsive design, and system fonts for performance.

doc.title: Story 10.1: Landing Page Redesign
doc.section: Hero section, format badges, visual design
doc.snippet: Redesigned landing page with hero section and visual format badges (NP3, XMP, lrtemplate, Capture One, DCP). Clean typography, modern layout, responsive design across devices.

doc.title: Story 10.2: Batch File Upload
doc.section: File cards, upload grid, drag-drop zone
doc.snippet: Batch file upload with drag-and-drop support. File cards displayed in grid layout with filename, format badge, file size. Upload manager class handles multiple files.

doc.title: Story 10.3: Progress Indicators
doc.section: Status updates, download buttons, error handling
doc.snippet: Progress indicators for batch conversions with per-file status (queued, processing, complete, error). Download buttons appear after successful conversion.

doc.title: Story 10.4: Individual File Conversion Controls
doc.section: Per-file format selection, individual convert buttons
doc.snippet: Per-file conversion controls with format selection dropdown and individual convert buttons. Supports retry for failed conversions and individual file cancellation.

file.reason: Existing responsive design implementation from Epic 2 (Story 2-10). Contains mobile-first base styles, tablet breakpoint (@media min-width: 768px), desktop breakpoint (@media min-width: 1024px), and touch-specific styles (@media hover: none). Will be extended for Epic 10 components.

Touch detection module from Story 2-10. Exports isTouchDevice(), adaptForTouch(), handleOrientationChange(), initializeResponsive(). Provides touch device detection and mobile-specific UI adaptations that will integrate with Epic 10 features.

file.symbol: Main structure with viewport meta tag
file.reason: Current web interface structure with viewport meta tag already present from Story 2-1. Contains hero section, drop zone, status displays that will be made responsive for Epic 10 batch upload features.

file.symbol: Application entry point with responsive init
file.reason: Main initialization includes responsive.js module import and initialization (line 14, 31). Shows integration pattern for responsive features across all Epic 10 components.

story-context.constraints: - Architecture: Must maintain mobile-first CSS approach established in Story 2-10 (base mobile, tablet @media min-width: 768px, desktop @media min-width: 1024px) - Performance: Must maintain <2 seconds load time on 3G connection (WebPageTest validation required) - Browser Support: Chrome 90+, Firefox 88+, Safari 14+, Edge 90+ (modern browsers only, no IE11) - Accessibility: WCAG AA compliance minimum (16px body text, 1.5 line-height, 4.5:1 contrast, 44px touch targets) - Touch Targets: iOS Human Interface Guidelines (minimum 44px x 44px, 48px recommended for primary actions) - No External Dependencies: System fonts only (no web fonts), vanilla CSS (no frameworks), zero CDN dependencies - Breakpoints: 320px (iPhone SE minimum), 768px (tablet), 1024px (desktop), 1920px (tested maximum) - Responsive Images: max-width: 100%, height: auto for all images/SVGs - No Horizontal Scrolling: overflow-x: hidden, test on 320px width minimum - Progressive Enhancement: Works without JavaScript for basic file download (fallback)

tests.standards: Recipe uses manual browser testing for web UI stories (no automated JavaScript testing framework). Testing standards for responsive design: (1) Manual testing across browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge) at all breakpoints (320px, 768px, 1024px, 1920px), (2) Real device testing required (iPhone, Android, iPad) - browser DevTools responsive mode insufficient for touch interactions, (3) Accessibility validation (keyboard navigation, screen reader compatibility, WCAG AA contrast), (4) Performance validation (WebPageTest on 3G, Lighthouse audit ≥95 score), (5) Visual regression via manual inspection (no screenshot comparison tools). Touch interactions must be tested on physical devices (tap vs click behaves differently).

locations.location: web/index.html - Manual browser testing at multiple screen sizes
web/css/main.css, layout.css, components.css - Visual regression via browser inspection
WebPageTest - Performance testing (3G load time validation)
Lighthouse CI - Accessibility and performance scores

test-case.title: Mobile layout (<768px) single column
test-case.description: Verify all content stacks vertically on mobile (iPhone SE 320px, iPhone 13 Pro 390px). No horizontal scrolling, tap-to-browse upload, full-width buttons.
test-case.verification: Manual testing on real iPhone devices (iOS Safari). Resize browser to 375px width. Check: single column layout, full-width buttons, readable text (16px minimum), tap targets 44px+.

test-case.title: Tablet layout (768px-1023px) two columns
test-case.description: Verify two-column grid for file cards, 2x3 format badge grid, optimized hero section on iPad (820px-834px width).
test-case.verification: Manual testing on real iPad devices (Safari). Check: two-column file grid, 2x3 badge grid, hero title 36px, touch targets 44px+.

test-case.title: Desktop layout (>1024px) three columns
test-case.description: Verify three-column grid for file cards, horizontal format badge row, full-size hero section on desktop (1920px width).
test-case.verification: Manual testing in Chrome/Firefox/Safari/Edge at 1920x1080. Check: three-column file grid, horizontal badge row, hero title 48px.

test-case.title: Touch-friendly tap targets (44px minimum)
test-case.description: Verify all interactive elements meet iOS guidelines (44px x 44px minimum). Primary actions 48px+ recommended. 8px spacing between targets.
test-case.verification: Manual testing on iPhone/Android. Use finger (not stylus) to tap buttons. Check: easy to tap without missing, no accidental taps on adjacent elements.

test-case.title: No horizontal scrolling at any breakpoint
test-case.description: Verify content fits viewport width at all sizes (320px to 1920px). No overflow-x, images scale to container, long filenames truncate.
test-case.verification: Manual testing: Resize browser from 320px to 1920px. Scroll vertically - no horizontal scrollbar should appear. Test with long filename uploads.

test-case.title: Readable text without zooming (WCAG AA)
test-case.description: Verify body text 16px minimum, small text 14px minimum, line-height 1.5, color contrast 4.5:1 for body text.
test-case.verification: Manual testing: Load on iPhone, verify text readable without pinch-zoom. Use browser DevTools contrast checker for WCAG validation.

test-case.title: Manual testing on real devices
test-case.description: Test on iPhone (iOS Safari), Android (Chrome), iPad (Safari), Desktop browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge). Portrait and landscape orientations.
test-case.verification: Document test results in docs/testing/responsive-design-testing.md. Include device name, screen size, OS version, pass/fail for each AC, screenshots of any issues.

test-case.title: Load time <2 seconds on 3G
test-case.description: Verify initial page load (including WASM) completes in under 2 seconds on 3G connection (1.6 Mbps).
test-case.verification: Use WebPageTest with 3G profile. Check: Load time, First Contentful Paint (FCP), Time to Interactive (TTI). Target: FCP <3s, TTI <5s.

test-case.title: Lighthouse audit ≥95 scores
test-case.description: Verify Lighthouse scores: Performance ≥95, Accessibility ≥95, Best Practices ≥95.
test-case.verification: Run Lighthouse CI in Chrome DevTools. Check scores for mobile and desktop profiles. Fix any failing audits.
